skill.name: comprehensive-review-full-review
skill.description: Guides a phased, multi-agent review of code quality, architecture, security, performance, tests, documentation, and DevOps practices.
skill.summary: Coordinate comprehensive code reviews across quality, security, performance, testing, documentation, and delivery practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, C2 keywords, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and security review terminology, with no executable code, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Reference
low_findings.description: The static shell execution finding at SKILL.md line 21 is a Markdown file reference, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. It instructs the assistant to open a playbook if examples are needed and does not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains inline Markdown around a resource path only. No shell interpreter, command string, or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Review Terminology
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md lines 3, 47, 49, 50, 59, and 81 are review prompts and headings. They ask reviewers to assess cryptographic implementation and architecture quality, but do not implement cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are natural-language review instructions. There is no code that selects MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any other weak primitive.

low_findings.title: False Positive: C2 Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword finding at SKILL.md line 110 appears to match SOC2 compliance text. This is a compliance review category, not command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 110 lists GDPR, PCI DSS, and SOC2 as compliance examples. No networking, beaconing, remote host, or control channel behavior is described.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Test Review Language
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding at SKILL.md line 75 is a prompt to evaluate testing strategy and coverage. It does not instruct collection of host, process, network, or environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is test quality review. No reconnaissance command, filesystem inventory, credential discovery, or environment enumeration is present.

content.value_statement: Large code reviews often miss issues across security, tests, performance, and operations. This skill structures a phased review that produces prioritized findings and remediation guidance.
actual_capabilities: Defines phased review coverage for code quality, architecture, security, performance, testing, documentation, and DevOps.
Provides specialized agent prompts for each review phase.
Supports optional focus areas such as security, performance, TDD, framework checks, and strict review mode.
Describes expected outputs for quality metrics, vulnerability reports, coverage gaps, and deployment assessments.
Consolidates findings into critical, high, medium, and low priority groups.
limitations: It is an instruction-only skill and does not include executable scanners or integrations.
Some referenced subagent names may not exist in every AI coding environment.
Results depend on the codebase access and tool availability provided by the user.
The referenced resources playbook is not included in this package.

use_cases.title: Pre-release Engineering Review
use_cases.description: Run a broad review before a release to identify quality, security, performance, and deployment risks.

use_cases.title: Security-focused Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Assess high-risk changes for OWASP issues, dependency exposure, secrets, and authentication flaws.

use_cases.title: Technical Debt Assessment
use_cases.description: Map architecture concerns, code smells, missing tests, and documentation gaps into a prioritized plan.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Pull Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a focused change set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review the current pull request. Focus on correctness, maintainability, security risks, and missing tests. Return prioritized findings with file references.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Security-focused Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use --security-focus for this codebase. Check authentication, authorization, input validation, dependency risk, secrets exposure, and risky configuration. Separate confirmed issues from recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Release Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use --performance-critical and --metrics-report for this release candidate. Review scalability, test coverage, documentation accuracy, CI/CD controls, and rollback readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a Full Architecture Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced multi-phase review for a large system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full review workflow for this repository. Include architecture, security, performance, tests, documentation, framework practices, and DevOps. Consolidate findings by release priority.

output_examples.input: Review a payment service before release.
output: Critical: Authentication bypass risk in refund approval flow requires immediate remediation.
High: Missing integration tests for payment failure paths should block release approval.
Medium: Documentation does not describe rollback steps for payment processor outages.

output_examples.input: Assess a React and Django application for technical debt.
output: Architecture: Frontend state management is split across competing patterns and needs consolidation.
Performance: Several list views risk slow rendering under larger customer accounts.
Testing: Backend authorization rules need focused coverage for role transitions.

best_practices: Provide the repository, target branch, release context, and review goals before starting.
Run focused options for high-risk areas such as security, performance, or TDD compliance.
Ask for evidence, severity, impact, and remediation steps for every confirmed finding.
anti_patterns: Do not use the workflow as a replacement for running real tests and scanners.
Do not accept broad findings without file references or concrete impact.
Do not run every phase when a small targeted review is sufficient.

faq.question: Does this skill execute security scanners?
faq.answer: No. It provides review prompts and workflow structure. Actual scanners depend on the tools available in your environment.

faq.question: Can it review only one pull request?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the pull request scope and ask for a focused review instead of the full workflow.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Tool behavior depends on available agents and integrations.

faq.question: Is it suitable for security reviews?
faq.answer: Yes, it includes a security-focused phase for OWASP issues, secrets, dependencies, and authorization concerns.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the codebase, target change, framework, risk areas, release timeline, and any required compliance standards.

faq.question: Will it fix the issues it finds?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to identify and prioritize issues. You can ask your coding assistant to implement selected fixes afterward.
